sections.paragraph:
ἀποθήκη, ης, ἡ (s. ἀποτίθημι; Thu. 6, 97, 5+; SIG 1106, 84; PRyl 97, 11; PTebt 347, 1; 5; BGU 32, 3; 816, 5; 931, 2; LXX; ApcEsdr 5:23 p. 30, 26 Tdf.; Jos., Ant. 9, 274; loanw. in rabb.) of a place where one puts someth. storehouse, barn συνάγειν εἰς τὴν ἀ. gather into the barn Mt 3:12; 6:26; 13:30; Lk 3:17 (Just., D.49, 3). S. on ἀλοάω.—Lk 12:18; w. ταμιεῖον (Jos., loc. cit.) 12:24; cellar for oil and wine Hm 11:15.—B. 492. M-M.

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

someth. someth. = something

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

loc. cit loc. cit = loco citato (in the place already cited)

Hm Hm = Mandates

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 110
